name: Denjin Charge Hadoken
notes: 2-hit projectile; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit; only comes out when the HP button is pressed

name: Denjin Charge Hadoken
notes: 3-hit OD projectile; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit

name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
notes: Version canceled from 6HK; no follow-up juggle like after jumping Air Tatsu; the last frame of recovery puts Ryu in a crouching state; useful juggle ender after midscreen OD High Blade Kick for damage, corner carry, and oki

name: Aerial Tatsumaki Senpu-kyaku
notes: Version canceled from 6HK; can hit crouching opponents unlike the jumping version; puts opponent into limited juggle state (follow-up only possible near the corner); the last frame of recovery puts Ryu in a crouching state

name: Denjin Charge Hashogeki
notes: () refers to recovery on block; strike hitbox but can nullify 2-hit meterless projectile; puts opponent into spinning juggle state on hit; only comes out when the Heavy Punch button is pressed

name: Denjin Hadoken
notes: 8-hit Super projectile; button strength does not affect projectile speed; opponent cannot jump over projectile after Super freeze if within ~1/2 screen; consumes Denjin stock for slightly more damage and KD Advantage; can be performed even when an OD Hadoken is on screen

name: Shin Hashogeki Lv.2
notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; invincibility can be reduced to 1-7f if the button is held the minimum length of time for Lv.2 charge (6f after Super freeze); strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level

name: Denjin Hashogeki Lv.2
notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; invincibility can be reduced to 1-7f if the button is held the minimum length of time for Lv.2 charge (6f after Super freeze); strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level; consumes Denjin stock for slightly more damage; Denjin version gives the tumbling OTG state that can lead to follow-up juggles (this is the only version that incurs damage scaling in combos)

name: Shin Hashogeki Lv.3
notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; full charge causes tumble OTG state; strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level; extremely high pushback on block (can be difficult to punish without holding Parry into immediate Drive Rush)

name: Denjin Hashogeki Lv.3
notes: Loses 4 invincibility frames if held; full charge causes tumble OTG state; strike hitbox; Burnout chip damage does not changed based on charge level; extremely high pushback on block (can be difficult to punish without holding Parry into immediate Drive Rush); consumes Denjin stock for slightly more damage
